concept: Stink / A Smell Gone Wrong / What the Nose Refuses
description: To give off a foul smell: food gone bad, a crowded room, anything the nose reads as wrong before the mind can say why. The negative-valence partner to whinu (smell, neutral), needed the moment a transmutation has to describe something honestly unpleasant rather than talk around it.
sound_symbolism: 'Po' opens with a stop, the nose's own recoil. 'Thu', breath shaped rough through an open mouth, the smell carried on the same air a clean scent would use, refused instead of welcomed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'sulopa pothu' (the soup stinks / has gone bad). Can take a source: 'lue thena pothu' (it stinks from the thing). The event noun, a stink, comes by rule.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Knowing what has spoiled is part of ecological literacy, not a fact to be softened.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A nose that can tell rot from freshness is old, embodied knowledge, the same kind that reads weather in a wind or a season in a smell.
